rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM JELLYMAN VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Campbell Industries Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Campbell Industries' motion for summary judgment, or in the alternative, summary adjudication is off calendar. Dismissal filed June 5, 2018. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM JELLYMAN VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Ferro Engineering Division Of On Marine Services Company, Llcs A Delaware Limited Liability Corporation (Erroneously Sued As On Marine Services Company Llc) Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2018 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Ferro Engineering Division of On Marine Services Company, LLC, a Delaware Limited Liability Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. Statements made during the deposition of William Jellyman preclude Defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312.= (503/CML)

rulings.Case Title: LISA EBBESEN ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, June 14, 2018 in Department 503, Line 3. Defendant J.S. Guerin & Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that Plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent Jeffrey Ebbesen was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to Defendant. Plaintiffs' interrogatory responses are not factually devoid under Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30 a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. =(503/CML)
